rulings.Case Title: STEVEN M ROBERTSON VS. JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., JOSEPH TORRANO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend address issues raised by demurrer, including alleging facts re: (1) loan at issue is "residential mortgage loan" within the meaning of CCC 2923.1; (2) tolling of statute of limitations; (3) assumption of liability; and (4) formation of a valid oral contact. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED RIBHI AYESH VS. SUKHDEVJI D. PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Claim Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, line 7. DEFENDANT SUKHDEVJI PATEL's Motion To Claim Attorney Fees DENIED. "A party who partially prevails on an anti-SLAPP motion must generally be considered a prevailing party unless the results of the motion were so insignificant that the party did not achieve any practical benefit from bringing the motion." Mann v. Quality Old Time Serv., Inc. (2006)139 Cal. App. 4th 328, 340. The only cause of action stricken pursuant to Defendant's motion was for declaratory relief. Contrary to Defendant's argument tin Reply, that the scope of litigation was narrowed because the issue of "whether Defendant forfeits the right to said taxes was removed from the case" (Reply 4:16-18), Defendant concedes in his moving papers for Motion to strike that defendant's liability, i.e. whether defendant has to pay taxes can be resolved by the cause of action for breach of contract. (Anti-SLAPP Motion 12:24-27). Therefore, net result remains the same, specifically, whether Defendant had to pay taxes when they were demanded. = (501/AJR)
